name: world-order
description:
Henry Kissinger's World Order — an executable toolkit for understanding global geopolitics: how nations interact, the balance of power, diplomatic strategy, and the forces shaping international order.

Covers 5 use cases:
① Understanding Geopolitics — grasp the fundamental forces shaping relations between nations
② The Balance of Power — analyze how equilibrium prevents war
③ Diplomatic Strategy — apply frameworks for negotiation and statecraft
④ Regional Dynamics — understand distinct worldviews of Europe, Middle East, Asia, America
⑤ Technology & World Order — analyze how technology is transforming global power.

Trigger when users say: "International relations" "Geopolitics" "World order" "Henry Kissinger" "Balance of power" "Foreign policy" "Diplomacy" "Geopolitical strategy" "Global politics" "How nations behave" "Cold War strategy" "Power politics" "Statecraft"
or mention: Henry Kissinger / World Order / geopolitics / balance of power / diplomacy / international relations / sovereignty / foreign policy.

Related skills: the-tragedy-of-great-power-politics, richard-nixon, leadership-in-turbulent-times, the-essential-drucker, the-lords-of-easy-money.
useCase: Helps anyone understand the forces shaping global politics — from historical foundations of international order to contemporary challenges of technology and multipolar power.
knownRisks: Kissinger's realist perspective is controversial; this skill presents his framework without endorsing all his policy positions.
